{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''城山神社'''（きやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}府中町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 ; 主祭神 :* {{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}（かみくしわけのみこと） :: 文献によっては「神櫛王（かみくしのみこ）」とも表記される。第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の{{jalink|皇子|Q12886381}}で、{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}の祖。 ; 合祀神 :* {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} :* {{jalink|天御中主神}} === 歴史 === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|明神原遺跡}}]] === 創建 === 社伝では、祭神の{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}は、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の御代に南海で悪魚を退治した功で{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}に任じられたという。のち館を城山に造営して住んでいたが、{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}8年に120歳で没した。その後、祠を建てその神霊を祀ったのが当社の始まりと伝える『讃岐国官社考證』（『香川県の地名』城山神社項より）。神社由緒書。。 {{jalink|神櫛別命}}に関しては、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』にも「{{jalink|神櫛王}}（神櫛別命に同じ）は{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}の祖である」と記載されている『日本書紀』景行天皇4年条「次妃五十河媛、生神櫛皇子・稲背入彦皇子。其兄神櫛皇子、是讃岐国造之始祖也。」。。なお『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「{{jalink|国造本紀|Q133833194}}」では「神櫛別命三世孫の{{jalink|須売保礼命}}が{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}期に讃岐国造の任を賜った」と記しており『国造本紀』（『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』第10巻）讃岐国造条「{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|軽島豊明朝}}御代。景行帝児神櫛王三世孫須売保礼命定賜国造。」。、{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}一族の祖とする点では同じである。 また、社伝では元々は{{jalink|城山 (香川県)||城山}}一峰に残る明神原遺跡（{{jalink|城山神社 (坂出市)#関係地|Q11426120|後述}}）の地に鎮座していたとしている。明神原には現在も巨石数個があることから、古代より同地で祭祀が行われたと推測される『香川県の地名』城山神社項。。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）条・{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）条に「城山神」に対する{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}昇叙の記事が見える。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|阿野郡|Q11658001}}に「城山神社 名神大」と記載され、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列している。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}の{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}は、当社のほか{{jalink|田村神社|Q11576976}}・{{jalink|粟井神社|Q11604560}}の計3社のみになる。このように崇敬を受けたのは城山山麓に{{jalink|讃岐国分寺|Q1101226|讃岐国府}}が置かれていたことも関係していたと見られ、府内鎮護・国衙守護の性格を持ったとする説もある。 『菅家文草』によれば、{{jalink|仁和|Q1346317}}4年（{{jalink|888年|Q29956}}）に{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682|讃岐国司}}の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は城山神社で降雨祈願を行なっている『{{jalink|菅家文草|Q109359816}}』仁和4年5月6日祭城山神文（[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html 城山神社]（神道・神社史料集成）参照）。。祈願は成功し豊作をもたらしたという。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の様子は不明であるが次第に衰退したと見られ、その後{{jalink|貞治|Q1146153}}元年（{{jalink|1362年|Q6166}}）の{{jalink|細川頼之|Q1139739}}・{{jalink|細川清氏|Q11605934|清氏}}の合戦の兵火で焼失した。そのため神体のみが印鑰の地（{{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=印鑰（一時遷座地）}}）に移されて小祠が建てられ、のち現在地に移された。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）11月7日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「城山神」。 * 貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）10月9日、従五位上 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「城山神」。 === 境内 === ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 社殿.JPG|社殿 ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 拝殿正面.JPG|拝殿 == 摂末社 == '''境内末社''' [[ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 境内雨請天満宮.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|雨請天満宮}}]] * 雨請天満宮 *: 祭神は{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公。{{jalink|仁和|Q1346317}}4年の道真の{{jalink|雨乞い|Q477738}}を感謝して祀ったことに始まる。 * 四神殿 '''境外末社''' * 福宮神社 * 御霊神社 * 金刀比羅宮 * 須佐之男社 * 市杵島社 * 砂野神社 === 関係地 === * {{jalink|城山 (香川県)||城山}}（きやま） *: {{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}と{{jalink|丸亀市|Q460835}}の間に位置する標高462mの山で、城山神社の背後にそびえる。山上には{{jalink|古代山城|Q6431764}}（{{jalink|城山城 (讃岐国)|Q46999719|讃岐城山城}}）が築かれたとされ、「城山」の山名はそれに由来する。一帯で城の遺構が見られ、国の史跡に指定されている[http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/nigiwai/kiyama.html 城山（きやま）]、[http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkashinkou/kiyama.html 史跡城山]（坂出市ホームページ）。。また、山頂付近からは{{jalink|打製石器|Q1142470|石器}}も発掘されており『香川県の地名』城山項。、古くから生活が営まれていたことがわかっている。下記の明神原（明神ヶ原）遺跡のように祭祀の場ともされていた。 * 明神原遺跡 ** 所在地：城山の南東の峰（{{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=明神原遺跡（旧鎮座地）}}） *: 城山神社の旧鎮座地と伝えられる。巨石が点在していることから、古来から神が降り立つ{{jalink|磐境}}として神聖視された場所と見られ、古くから祭祀が行われていたと推測されている。{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}が当地で雨乞いを行ったとも伝える。 * {{jalink|神櫛皇子#墓|Q11590432|神櫛王墓}} ** 所在地：{{jalink|高松市|Q200707}}牟礼町牟礼（{{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=神櫛王公式墓}}） *: 宮内庁の所管する{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛王}}治定墓。 * {{jalink|鼓岡神社|Q11678981}} ** 所在地：坂出市府中町（{{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=鼓岡神社（神櫛王墓か）}}） *: 城山神社社伝では、鼓岡神社の地は{{jalink|前方後円墳|Q11268718}}であり{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}の墳墓であるともいう、とする『香川県の地名』鼓岡項では{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}と記載（神櫛別命との関係性は指摘していない）。。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}府中町本村4760 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531}}（JR四国）{{jalink|予讃線|Q575016}}（{{jalink|瀬戸大橋線|Q7456750}}） {{jalink|讃岐府中駅|Q5115413}} （徒歩約20分） '''周辺''' * 讃岐国府跡 * {{jalink|鼓岡神社|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|崇徳天皇|Q349287|崇徳上皇}}行在所伝承地。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 香川県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）坂出市 城山項・城山神社項 * 香川県神職会 編『香川県神社誌』下巻,香川県神職会,昭13. 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (参照 2025-06-30) === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|城山城 (讃岐国)|Q46999719}} * {{jalink|櫛梨神社}}（{{jalink|仲多度郡|Q1206155}}{{jalink|琴平町|Q1271483}}下櫛梨） - {{jalink|式内社}}。祭神に同じく神櫛王命を祀る。 === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * 旧{{jalink|県社}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}8年 |本殿 = {{jalink|春日造|Q3193781}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月8日|Q2924}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''城山神社'''（きやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}府中町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|県社}}。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の3柱。 ; 主祭神 :* {{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}（かみくしわけのみこと） :: 文献によっては「神櫛王（かみくしのみこ）」とも表記される。第12代{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の{{jalink|皇子|Q12886381}}で、{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}の祖。 ; 合祀神 :* {{jalink|天照大神|Q455602}} :* {{jalink|天御中主神}} === 歴史 === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|明神原遺跡}}]] === 創建 === 社伝では、祭神の{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}は、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の御代に南海で悪魚を退治した功で{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}に任じられたという。のち館を城山に造営して住んでいたが、{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}8年に120歳で没した。その後、祠を建てその神霊を祀ったのが当社の始まりと伝える『讃岐国官社考證』（『香川県の地名』城山神社項より）。神社由緒書。。 {{jalink|神櫛別命}}に関しては、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』にも「{{jalink|神櫛王}}（神櫛別命に同じ）は{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}の祖である」と記載されている『日本書紀』景行天皇4年条「次妃五十河媛、生神櫛皇子・稲背入彦皇子。其兄神櫛皇子、是讃岐国造之始祖也。」。。なお『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「{{jalink|国造本紀|Q133833194}}」では「神櫛別命三世孫の{{jalink|須売保礼命}}が{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}期に讃岐国造の任を賜った」と記しており『国造本紀』（『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』第10巻）讃岐国造条「{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|軽島豊明朝}}御代。景行帝児神櫛王三世孫須売保礼命定賜国造。」。、{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}一族の祖とする点では同じである。 また、社伝では元々は{{jalink|城山 (香川県)||城山}}一峰に残る明神原遺跡（{{jalink|城山神社 (坂出市)#関係地|Q11426120|後述}}）の地に鎮座していたとしている。明神原には現在も巨石数個があることから、古代より同地で祭祀が行われたと推測される『香川県の地名』城山神社項。。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）条・{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）条に「城山神」に対する{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}昇叙の記事が見える。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|阿野郡|Q11658001}}に「城山神社 名神大」と記載され、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列している。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}の{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}は、当社のほか{{jalink|田村神社|Q11576976}}・{{jalink|粟井神社|Q11604560}}の計3社のみになる。このように崇敬を受けたのは城山山麓に{{jalink|讃岐国分寺|Q1101226|讃岐国府}}が置かれていたことも関係していたと見られ、府内鎮護・国衙守護の性格を持ったとする説もある。 『菅家文草』によれば、{{jalink|仁和|Q1346317}}4年（{{jalink|888年|Q29956}}）に{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682|讃岐国司}}の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は城山神社で降雨祈願を行なっている『{{jalink|菅家文草|Q109359816}}』仁和4年5月6日祭城山神文（[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html 城山神社]（神道・神社史料集成）参照）。。祈願は成功し豊作をもたらしたという。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の様子は不明であるが次第に衰退したと見られ、その後{{jalink|貞治|Q1146153}}元年（{{jalink|1362年|Q6166}}）の{{jalink|細川頼之|Q1139739}}・{{jalink|細川清氏|Q11605934|清氏}}の合戦の兵火で焼失した。そのため神体のみが印鑰の地（{{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=印鑰（一時遷座地）}}）に移されて小祠が建てられ、のち現在地に移された。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）11月7日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「城山神」。 * 貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）10月9日、従五位上 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「城山神」。 === 境内 === ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 社殿.JPG|社殿 ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 拝殿正面.JPG|拝殿 == 摂末社 == '''境内末社''' [[ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 境内雨請天満宮.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|雨請天満宮}}]] * 雨請天満宮 *: 祭神は{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公。{{jalink|仁和|Q1346317}}4年の道真の{{jalink|雨乞い|Q477738}}を感謝して祀ったことに始まる。 * 四神殿 '''境外末社''' * 福宮神社 * 御霊神社 * 金刀比羅宮 * 須佐之男社 * 市杵島社 * 砂野神社 === 関係地 === * {{jalink|城山 (香川県)||城山}}（きやま） *: {{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}と{{jalink|丸亀市|Q460835}}の間に位置する標高462mの山で、城山神社の背後にそびえる。山上には{{jalink|古代山城|Q6431764}}（{{jalink|城山城 (讃岐国)|Q46999719|讃岐城山城}}）が築かれたとされ、「城山」の山名はそれに由来する。一帯で城の遺構が見られ、国の史跡に指定されている[http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/nigiwai/kiyama.html 城山（きやま）]、[http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkashinkou/kiyama.html 史跡城山]（坂出市ホームページ）。。また、山頂付近からは{{jalink|打製石器|Q1142470|石器}}も発掘されており『香川県の地名』城山項。、古くから生活が営まれていたことがわかっている。下記の明神原（明神ヶ原）遺跡のように祭祀の場ともされていた。 * 明神原遺跡 ** 所在地：城山の南東の峰（{{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=明神原遺跡（旧鎮座地）}}） *: 城山神社の旧鎮座地と伝えられる。巨石が点在していることから、古来から神が降り立つ{{jalink|磐境}}として神聖視された場所と見られ、古くから祭祀が行われていたと推測されている。{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}が当地で雨乞いを行ったとも伝える。 * {{jalink|神櫛皇子#墓|Q11590432|神櫛王墓}} ** 所在地：{{jalink|高松市|Q200707}}牟礼町牟礼（{{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=神櫛王公式墓}}） *: 宮内庁の所管する{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛王}}治定墓。 * {{jalink|鼓岡神社|Q11678981}} ** 所在地：坂出市府中町（{{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=鼓岡神社（神櫛王墓か）}}） *: 城山神社社伝では、鼓岡神社の地は{{jalink|前方後円墳|Q11268718}}であり{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}の墳墓であるともいう、とする『香川県の地名』鼓岡項では{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}と記載（神櫛別命との関係性は指摘していない）。。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}府中町本村4760 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531}}（JR四国）{{jalink|予讃線|Q575016}}（{{jalink|瀬戸大橋線|Q7456750}}） {{jalink|讃岐府中駅|Q5115413}} （徒歩約20分） '''周辺''' * 讃岐国府跡 * {{jalink|鼓岡神社|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|崇徳天皇|Q349287|崇徳上皇}}行在所伝承地。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 香川県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）坂出市 城山項・城山神社項 * 香川県神職会 :* {{jalink|天御中主神}} === 歴史 === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|明神原遺跡}}]] === 創建 === 社伝では、祭神の{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}は、{{jalink|景行天皇|Q329723}}の御代に南海で悪魚を退治した功で{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}に任じられたという。のち館を城山に造営して住んでいたが、{{jalink|仲哀天皇|Q179971}}8年に120歳で没した。その後、祠を建てその神霊を祀ったのが当社の始まりと伝える『讃岐国官社考證』（『香川県の地名』城山神社項より）。神社由緒書。。 {{jalink|神櫛別命}}に関しては、『{{jalink|日本書紀|Q813935}}』にも「{{jalink|神櫛王}}（神櫛別命に同じ）は{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}の祖である」と記載されている『日本書紀』景行天皇4年条「次妃五十河媛、生神櫛皇子・稲背入彦皇子。其兄神櫛皇子、是讃岐国造之始祖也。」。。なお『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』「{{jalink|国造本紀|Q133833194}}」では「神櫛別命三世孫の{{jalink|須売保礼命}}が{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}}期に讃岐国造の任を賜った」と記しており『国造本紀』（『{{jalink|先代旧事本紀|Q2716751}}』第10巻）讃岐国造条「{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997|軽島豊明朝}}御代。景行帝児神櫛王三世孫須売保礼命定賜国造。」。、{{jalink|讃岐国造|Q21653200}}一族の祖とする点では同じである。 また、社伝では元々は{{jalink|城山 (香川県)||城山}}一峰に残る明神原遺跡（{{jalink|城山神社 (坂出市)#関係地|Q11426120|後述}}）の地に鎮座していたとしている。明神原には現在も巨石数個があることから、古代より同地で祭祀が行われたと推測される『香川県の地名』城山神社項。。 === 概史 === {{jalink|六国史|Q1151168|国史}}では、『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）条・{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）条に「城山神」に対する{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}昇叙の記事が見える。 {{jalink|延長 (元号)|Q1327274|延長}}5年（{{jalink|927年|Q30470}}）成立の『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}{{jalink|阿野郡|Q11658001}}に「城山神社 名神大」と記載され、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列している。{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682}}の{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}は、当社のほか{{jalink|田村神社|Q11576976}}・{{jalink|粟井神社|Q11604560}}の計3社のみになる。このように崇敬を受けたのは城山山麓に{{jalink|讃岐国分寺|Q1101226|讃岐国府}}が置かれていたことも関係していたと見られ、府内鎮護・国衙守護の性格を持ったとする説もある。 『菅家文草』によれば、{{jalink|仁和|Q1346317}}4年（{{jalink|888年|Q29956}}）に{{jalink|讃岐国|Q929682|讃岐国司}}の{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}は城山神社で降雨祈願を行なっている『{{jalink|菅家文草|Q109359816}}』仁和4年5月6日祭城山神文（[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html 城山神社]（神道・神社史料集成）参照）。。祈願は成功し豊作をもたらしたという。 {{jalink|中世|Q12554}}の様子は不明であるが次第に衰退したと見られ、その後{{jalink|貞治|Q1146153}}元年（{{jalink|1362年|Q6166}}）の{{jalink|細川頼之|Q1139739}}・{{jalink|細川清氏|Q11605934|清氏}}の合戦の兵火で焼失した。そのため神体のみが印鑰の地（{{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=印鑰（一時遷座地）}}）に移されて小祠が建てられ、のち現在地に移された。 {{jalink|明治維新|Q8707}}後、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|県社}}に列した。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）11月7日、正六位上から従五位下 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「城山神」。 * 貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）10月9日、従五位上 （『日本三代実録』） - 表記は「城山神」。 === 境内 === ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 社殿.JPG|社殿 ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 拝殿正面.JPG|拝殿 == 摂末社 == '''境内末社''' [[ファイル:城山神社 (坂出市) 境内雨請天満宮.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|雨請天満宮}}]] * 雨請天満宮 *: 祭神は{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公。{{jalink|仁和|Q1346317}}4年の道真の{{jalink|雨乞い|Q477738}}を感謝して祀ったことに始まる。 * 四神殿 '''境外末社''' * 福宮神社 * 御霊神社 * 金刀比羅宮 * 須佐之男社 * 市杵島社 * 砂野神社 === 関係地 === * {{jalink|城山 (香川県)||城山}}（きやま） *: {{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}と{{jalink|丸亀市|Q460835}}の間に位置する標高462mの山で、城山神社の背後にそびえる。山上には{{jalink|古代山城|Q6431764}}（{{jalink|城山城 (讃岐国)|Q46999719|讃岐城山城}}）が築かれたとされ、「城山」の山名はそれに由来する。一帯で城の遺構が見られ、国の史跡に指定されている[http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/nigiwai/kiyama.html 城山（きやま）]、[http://www.city.sakaide.lg.jp/soshiki/bunkashinkou/kiyama.html 史跡城山]（坂出市ホームページ）。。また、山頂付近からは{{jalink|打製石器|Q1142470|石器}}も発掘されており『香川県の地名』城山項。、古くから生活が営まれていたことがわかっている。下記の明神原（明神ヶ原）遺跡のように祭祀の場ともされていた。 * 明神原遺跡 ** 所在地：城山の南東の峰（{{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=明神原遺跡（旧鎮座地）}}） *: 城山神社の旧鎮座地と伝えられる。巨石が点在していることから、古来から神が降り立つ{{jalink|磐境}}として神聖視された場所と見られ、古くから祭祀が行われていたと推測されている。{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}が当地で雨乞いを行ったとも伝える。 * {{jalink|神櫛皇子#墓|Q11590432|神櫛王墓}} ** 所在地：{{jalink|高松市|Q200707}}牟礼町牟礼（{{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=神櫛王公式墓}}） *: 宮内庁の所管する{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛王}}治定墓。 * {{jalink|鼓岡神社|Q11678981}} ** 所在地：坂出市府中町（{{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=鼓岡神社（神櫛王墓か）}}） *: 城山神社社伝では、鼓岡神社の地は{{jalink|前方後円墳|Q11268718}}であり{{jalink|神櫛皇子|Q11590432|神櫛別命}}の墳墓であるともいう、とする『香川県の地名』鼓岡項では{{jalink|円墳|Q11394747}}と記載（神櫛別命との関係性は指摘していない）。。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|香川県|Q161454}}{{jalink|坂出市|Q855059}}府中町本村4760 '''交通アクセス''' * 鉄道：{{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531}}（JR四国）{{jalink|予讃線|Q575016}}（{{jalink|瀬戸大橋線|Q7456750}}） {{jalink|讃岐府中駅|Q5115413}} （徒歩約20分） '''周辺''' * 讃岐国府跡 * {{jalink|鼓岡神社|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|崇徳天皇|Q349287|崇徳上皇}}行在所伝承地。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * 『日本歴史地名大系 香川県の地名』（{{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}）坂出市 城山項・城山神社項 * 香川県神職会 House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}8 years |Main shrine = {{jalink|Kasugazo|Q3193781}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 8th|Q2924}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{jalink|prince|prince|Q12886381}} of the 12th {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} and the ancestor of {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} :* {{jalink|Tenminakanushikami}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} was appointed {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{jalink|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}, "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" also says, "{{jalink|Kamikushio no Mikoto}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{jalink|Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751}}" and "{{jalink|Kokuzohonki|Q133833194}}," "{{jalink|Suri Hore no Mikoto}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{jalink|Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Karushima Toyomincho}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{jalink|Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|described later}}) which remains on one peak of {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 1st Year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}{{jalink|Ano District|Q11658001}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} in {{jalink|Sanuki Country|Q929682}}: {{jalink|Tamura Shrine|Q11576976}} and {{jalink|Awai Shrine|Q11604560}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{jalink|Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|Sanuki Kokufu}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}}4 ({{jalink|888|Q29956}}), {{jalink|Sugawara Michizane|Q382005}} of {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682|Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{jalink|Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{jalink|Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739}} and {{jalink|Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|Kiyoshi}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{jalink|Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}} It began with Michizane's {{jalink|rain praying|Q477738}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}} and {{jalink|Marugame City|Q460835}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{jalink|ancient mountain castle|Q6431764}} ({{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|Sanuki Shiroyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{jalink|hammered stone tools|Q1142470|stone tools}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} prayed for rain here. * {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|King Kamikushi Tomb}} ** Location: {{jalink|Takamatsu City|Q200707}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Prince Kamikushi}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|Shikoku Railway|Q496531}} (JR Shikoku) {{jalink|Yosan Line|Q575016}} ({{jalink|Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750}}) {{jalink|Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|Retired Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719}} * {{jalink|Kushinashi Shrine}} ({{jalink|Nakatado District|Q1206155}}{{jalink|Kotohira Town|Q1271483}}Shimokushiri) - {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kiyama Shinshiya Sakai Teishi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqq]]

House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}8 years |Main shrine = {{jalink|Kasugazo|Q3193781}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 8th|Q2924}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{jalink|prince|prince|Q12886381}} of the 12th {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} and the ancestor of {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} :* {{jalink|Tenminakanushikami}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} was appointed {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{jalink|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}, "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" also says, "{{jalink|Kamikushio no Mikoto}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{jalink|Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751}}" and "{{jalink|Kokuzohonki|Q133833194}}," "{{jalink|Suri Hore no Mikoto}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{jalink|Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Karushima Toyomincho}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{jalink|Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|described later}}) which remains on one peak of {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 1st Year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}{{jalink|Ano District|Q11658001}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} in {{jalink|Sanuki Country|Q929682}}: {{jalink|Tamura Shrine|Q11576976}} and {{jalink|Awai Shrine|Q11604560}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{jalink|Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|Sanuki Kokufu}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}}4 ({{jalink|888|Q29956}}), {{jalink|Sugawara Michizane|Q382005}} of {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682|Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{jalink|Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{jalink|Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739}} and {{jalink|Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|Kiyoshi}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{jalink|Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}} It began with Michizane's {{jalink|rain praying|Q477738}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}} and {{jalink|Marugame City|Q460835}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{jalink|ancient mountain castle|Q6431764}} ({{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|Sanuki Shiroyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. In addition, {{jalink|hammered stone tools|Q1142470|stone tools}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. It is also said that {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} prayed for rain here. * {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|King Kamikushi Tomb}} ** Location: {{jalink|Takamatsu City|Q200707}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Prince Kamikushi}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|Shikoku Railway|Q496531}} (JR Shikoku) {{jalink|Yosan Line|Q575016}} ({{jalink|Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750}}) {{jalink|Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|Retired Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719}} * {{jalink|Kushinashi Shrine}} ({{jalink|Nakatado District|Q1206155}}{{jalink|Kotohira Town|Q1271483}}Shimokushiri) - {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kiyama Shinshiya Sakai Teishi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqq]]

https://web.archive.org/web/20121223073454/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/550401.html == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183230 ** P3831: 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior == * {{ill|Mount Kiyama|lt=Mount Kiyama|ceb|Ki Yama|WD=Q31699082}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} [[JA:城山神社 (坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}8 years |Main shrine = {{jalink|Kasugazo|Q3193781}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 8th|Q2924}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{jalink|prince|prince|Q12886381}} of the 12th {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} and the ancestor of {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} :* {{jalink|Tenminakanushikami}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} was appointed {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{jalink|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}, "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" also says, "{{jalink|Kamikushio no Mikoto}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{jalink|Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751}}" and "{{jalink|Kokuzohonki|Q133833194}}," "{{jalink|Suri Hore no Mikoto}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{jalink|Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Karushima Toyomincho}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{jalink|Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|described later}}) which remains on one peak of {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 1st Year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}{{jalink|Ano District|Q11658001}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} in {{jalink|Sanuki Country|Q929682}}: {{jalink|Tamura Shrine|Q11576976}} and {{jalink|Awai Shrine|Q11604560}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{jalink|Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|Sanuki Kokufu}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}}4 ({{jalink|888|Q29956}}), {{jalink|Sugawara Michizane|Q382005}} of {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682|Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{jalink|Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{jalink|Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739}} and {{jalink|Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|Kiyoshi}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{jalink|Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}} It began with Michizane's {{jalink|rain praying|Q477738}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}} and {{jalink|Marugame City|Q460835}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{jalink|ancient mountain castle|Q6431764}} ({{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|Sanuki Shiroyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{jalink|hammered stone tools|Q1142470|stone tools}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} prayed for rain here. * {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|King Kamikushi Tomb}} ** Location: {{jalink|Takamatsu City|Q200707}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Prince Kamikushi}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|Shikoku Railway|Q496531}} (JR Shikoku) {{jalink|Yosan Line|Q575016}} ({{jalink|Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750}}) {{jalink|Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|Retired Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719}} * {{jalink|Kushinashi Shrine}} ({{jalink|Nakatado District|Q1206155}}{{jalink|Kotohira Town|Q1271483}}Shimokushiri) - {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kiyama Shinshiya Sakai Teishi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqq]]

(坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Former {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}8 years |Main shrine = {{jalink|Kasugazo|Q3193781}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{jalink|October 8th|Q2924}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Prefectural shrine}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{jalink|prince|prince|Q12886381}} of the 12th {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} and the ancestor of {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{jalink|Amaterasu|Q455602}} :* {{jalink|Tenminakanushikami}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} was appointed {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} during the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Keiko|Q329723}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{jalink|Emperor Chuai|Q179971}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{jalink|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}, "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" also says, "{{jalink|Kamikushio no Mikoto}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{jalink|Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751}}" and "{{jalink|Kokuzohonki|Q133833194}}," "{{jalink|Suri Hore no Mikoto}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{jalink|Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Karushima Toyomincho}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{jalink|Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|described later}}) which remains on one peak of {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 1st Year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}{{jalink|Ano District|Q11658001}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} in {{jalink|Sanuki Country|Q929682}}: {{jalink|Tamura Shrine|Q11576976}} and {{jalink|Awai Shrine|Q11604560}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{jalink|Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|Sanuki Kokufu}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}}4 ({{jalink|888|Q29956}}), {{jalink|Sugawara Michizane|Q382005}} of {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682|Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{jalink|Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{jalink|Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739}} and {{jalink|Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|Kiyoshi}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{jalink|Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}} It began with Michizane's {{jalink|rain praying|Q477738}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}} and {{jalink|Marugame City|Q460835}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{jalink|ancient mountain castle|Q6431764}} ({{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|Sanuki Shiroyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{jalink|hammered stone tools|Q1142470|stone tools}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} prayed for rain here. * {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|King Kamikushi Tomb}} ** Location: {{jalink|Takamatsu City|Q200707}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Prince Kamikushi}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|Shikoku Railway|Q496531}} (JR Shikoku) {{jalink|Yosan Line|Q575016}} ({{jalink|Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750}}) {{jalink|Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|Retired Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719}} * {{jalink|Kushinashi Shrine}} ({{jalink|Nakatado District|Q1206155}}{{jalink|Kotohira Town|Q1271483}}Shimokushiri) - {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kiyama Shinshiya Sakai Teishi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqq]]

Shrine history book. .Regarding {{jalink|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}, "{{jalink|Nihon Shoki|Q813935}}" also says, "{{jalink|Kamikushio no Mikoto}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{jalink|Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751}}" and "{{jalink|Kokuzohonki|Q133833194}}," "{{jalink|Suri Hore no Mikoto}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{jalink|Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{jalink|Emperor Ojin|Q317997|Karushima Toyomincho}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{jalink|Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{jalink|Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|described later}}) which remains on one peak of {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{jalink|Rokukokushi|Q1151168|Kokushi}}, "{{jalink|Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981}}" {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} 1st Year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{jalink|extension (era name)|Q1327274|extended}} "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" established in 5 years ({{jalink|927 years|Q30470}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682}}{{jalink|Ano District|Q11658001}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} in {{jalink|Sanuki Country|Q929682}}: {{jalink|Tamura Shrine|Q11576976}} and {{jalink|Awai Shrine|Q11604560}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{jalink|Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|Sanuki Kokufu}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}}4 ({{jalink|888|Q29956}}), {{jalink|Sugawara Michizane|Q382005}} of {{jalink|Sanuki Province|Q929682|Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{jalink|Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{jalink|medieval period|Q12554}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{jalink|Sadaharu|Q1146153}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{jalink|Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739}} and {{jalink|Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|Kiyoshi}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{jalink|Meiji Restoration|Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{jalink|prefectural shrine}} in {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. === God level === * {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jogan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{jalink|Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{jalink|865|Q30350}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}}. {{jalink|Ninwa|Q1346317}} It began with Michizane's {{jalink|rain praying|Q477738}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{jalink|Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}} and {{jalink|Marugame City|Q460835}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{jalink|ancient mountain castle|Q6431764}} ({{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|Sanuki Shiroyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{jalink|hammered stone tools|Q1142470|stone tools}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} prayed for rain here. * {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|King Kamikushi Tomb}} ** Location: {{jalink|Takamatsu City|Q200707}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{jalink|Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|Prince Kamikushi}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{jalink|keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{jalink|Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{jalink|circular tumulus|Q11394747}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454}}{{jalink|Sakaide City|Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{jalink|Shikoku Railway|Q496531}} (JR Shikoku) {{jalink|Yosan Line|Q575016}} ({{jalink|Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750}}) {{jalink|Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{jalink|Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981}} - {{jalink|Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|Retired Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{jalink|Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719}} * {{jalink|Kushinashi Shrine}} ({{jalink|Nakatado District|Q1206155}}{{jalink|Kotohira Town|Q1271483}}Shimokushiri) - {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kiyama Shinshiya Sakai Teishi}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqq]]

He was the {{ill|lt=prince|prince|WD=prince|lt=Q12886381}} of the 12th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602}} :* {{ill||lt=Tenminakanushikami|UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}} was appointed {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707}}, it was ranked as {{ill||lt=prefectural shrine|UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317}} It began with Michizane's {{ill|lt=rain praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)||WD=|lt=Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059}} and {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|WD=Q460835}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|WD=Q6431764}} ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|lt=Sanuki Shiroyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{ill|lt=hammered stone tools|Q1142470|WD=Q1142470|lt=stone tools}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} prayed for rain here. * {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=King Kamikushi Tomb}} ** Location: {{ill|lt=Takamatsu City|Q200707|WD=Q200707}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Prince Kamikushi}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{ill|lt=circular tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway|Q496531|WD=Q496531}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016}} ({{ill|lt=Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750|WD=Q7456750}}) {{ill|lt=Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413|WD=Q5115413}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981}} -
