{{ill|Mount Kiyama|lt=Mount Kiyama|ceb|Ki Yama|WD=Q31699082|1=Mount Kiyama}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} [[JA:城山神社 (坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}8 years |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=October 8th|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{ill|lt=prince|prince|WD=prince|ltq=Q12886381}} of the 12th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenminakanushikami|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} was appointed {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}" also says, "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushio no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}" and "{{ill|lt=Kokuzohonki|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}," "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suri Hore no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{ill|lt=Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Karushima Toyomincho|1=Emperor Ōjin}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|WD=Q11426120|lt=described later|1=Kiyama Shrine}}) which remains on one peak of {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st Year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ano District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Country|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}: {{ill|lt=Tamura Shrine|Q11576976|WD=Q11576976|1=Tamura Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awai Shrine|Q11604560|WD=Q11604560|1=Awai Shrine}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokufu|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}}4 ({{ill|lt=888|Q29956|WD=Q29956|1=AD 888}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{ill|lt=Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816|WD=Q109359816|1=Kanke Bunsō}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{ill|lt=Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|lt=Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|WD=Q11605934|lt=Kiyoshi|1=Hosokawa Kiyouji}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} It began with Michizane's {{ill|lt=rain praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} and {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|WD=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|WD=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}} ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|lt=Sanuki Shiroyama Castle|1=Kiyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{ill|lt=hammered stone tools|Q1142470|WD=Q1142470|lt=stone tools|1=Lithic reduction}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for rain here. * {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=King Kamikushi Tomb|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} ** Location: {{ill|lt=Takamatsu City|Q200707|WD=Q200707|1=Takamatsu}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Prince Kamikushi|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{ill|lt=circular tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} ({{ill|lt=Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750|WD=Q7456750|1=Seto-Ōhashi Line}}) {{ill|lt=Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413|WD=Q5115413|1=Sanuki-Fuchū Station}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} - {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|WD=Q349287|lt=Retired Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|1=Kiyama Castle}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nakatado District|Q1206155|WD=Q1206155|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kotohira Town|Q1271483|WD=Q1271483|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}Shimokushiri) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:坂出市の建築物]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

(P3225) == * 7470005003531 == Commons category (P373) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '香川県坂出市府中町本村4760', 'language': 'ja'} == location of formation (P740) == * {{ill|Mount Kiyama|lt=Mount Kiyama|ceb|Ki Yama|WD=Q31699082|1=Mount Kiyama}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} [[JA:城山神社 (坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}8 years |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=October 8th|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{ill|lt=prince|prince|WD=prince|ltq=Q12886381}} of the 12th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenminakanushikami|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} was appointed {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}" also says, "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushio no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}" and "{{ill|lt=Kokuzohonki|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}," "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suri Hore no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{ill|lt=Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Karushima Toyomincho|1=Emperor Ōjin}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|WD=Q11426120|lt=described later|1=Kiyama Shrine}}) which remains on one peak of {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st Year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ano District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Country|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}: {{ill|lt=Tamura Shrine|Q11576976|WD=Q11576976|1=Tamura Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awai Shrine|Q11604560|WD=Q11604560|1=Awai Shrine}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokufu|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}}4 ({{ill|lt=888|Q29956|WD=Q29956|1=AD 888}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{ill|lt=Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816|WD=Q109359816|1=Kanke Bunsō}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{ill|lt=Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|lt=Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|WD=Q11605934|lt=Kiyoshi|1=Hosokawa Kiyouji}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} It began with Michizane's {{ill|lt=rain praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} and {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|WD=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|WD=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}} ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|lt=Sanuki Shiroyama Castle|1=Kiyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{ill|lt=hammered stone tools|Q1142470|WD=Q1142470|lt=stone tools|1=Lithic reduction}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for rain here. * {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=King Kamikushi Tomb|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} ** Location: {{ill|lt=Takamatsu City|Q200707|WD=Q200707|1=Takamatsu}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Prince Kamikushi|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{ill|lt=circular tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} ({{ill|lt=Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750|WD=Q7456750|1=Seto-Ōhashi Line}}) {{ill|lt=Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413|WD=Q5115413|1=Sanuki-Fuchū Station}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} - {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|WD=Q349287|lt=Retired Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|1=Kiyama Castle}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nakatado District|Q1206155|WD=Q1206155|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kotohira Town|Q1271483|WD=Q1271483|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}Shimokushiri) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:坂出市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

(P3225) == * 7470005003531 == Commons category (P373) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '香川県坂出市府中町本村4760', 'language': 'ja'} == location of formation (P740) == * {{ill|Mount Kiyama|lt=Mount Kiyama|ceb|Ki Yama|WD=Q31699082|1=Mount Kiyama}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} [[JA:城山神社 (坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}8 years |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=October 8th|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{ill|lt=prince|prince|WD=prince|ltq=Q12886381}} of the 12th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenminakanushikami|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} was appointed {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}" also says, "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushio no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}" and "{{ill|lt=Kokuzohonki|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}," "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suri Hore no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{ill|lt=Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Karushima Toyomincho|1=Emperor Ōjin}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|WD=Q11426120|lt=described later|1=Kiyama Shrine}}) which remains on one peak of {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st Year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ano District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Country|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}: {{ill|lt=Tamura Shrine|Q11576976|WD=Q11576976|1=Tamura Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awai Shrine|Q11604560|WD=Q11604560|1=Awai Shrine}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokufu|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}}4 ({{ill|lt=888|Q29956|WD=Q29956|1=AD 888}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{ill|lt=Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816|WD=Q109359816|1=Kanke Bunsō}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{ill|lt=Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|lt=Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|WD=Q11605934|lt=Kiyoshi|1=Hosokawa Kiyouji}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} It began with Michizane's {{ill|lt=rain praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} and {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|WD=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|WD=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}} ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|lt=Sanuki Shiroyama Castle|1=Kiyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{ill|lt=hammered stone tools|Q1142470|WD=Q1142470|lt=stone tools|1=Lithic reduction}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for rain here. * {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=King Kamikushi Tomb|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} ** Location: {{ill|lt=Takamatsu City|Q200707|WD=Q200707|1=Takamatsu}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Prince Kamikushi|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{ill|lt=circular tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} ({{ill|lt=Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750|WD=Q7456750|1=Seto-Ōhashi Line}}) {{ill|lt=Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413|WD=Q5115413|1=Sanuki-Fuchū Station}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} - {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|WD=Q349287|lt=Retired Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|1=Kiyama Castle}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nakatado District|Q1206155|WD=Q1206155|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kotohira Town|Q1271483|WD=Q1271483|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}Shimokushiri) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:讃岐国の式内社|名きやましんしやさかいてし]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

{{ill|Mount Kiyama|lt=Mount Kiyama|ceb|Ki Yama|WD=Q31699082|1=Mount Kiyama}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} [[JA:城山神社 (坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}8 years |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=October 8th|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{ill|lt=prince|prince|WD=prince|ltq=Q12886381}} of the 12th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenminakanushikami|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} was appointed {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}" also says, "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushio no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}" and "{{ill|lt=Kokuzohonki|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}," "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suri Hore no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{ill|lt=Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Karushima Toyomincho|1=Emperor Ōjin}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|WD=Q11426120|lt=described later|1=Kiyama Shrine}}) which remains on one peak of {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st Year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ano District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Country|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}: {{ill|lt=Tamura Shrine|Q11576976|WD=Q11576976|1=Tamura Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awai Shrine|Q11604560|WD=Q11604560|1=Awai Shrine}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokufu|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}}4 ({{ill|lt=888|Q29956|WD=Q29956|1=AD 888}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{ill|lt=Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816|WD=Q109359816|1=Kanke Bunsō}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{ill|lt=Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|lt=Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|WD=Q11605934|lt=Kiyoshi|1=Hosokawa Kiyouji}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} It began with Michizane's {{ill|lt=rain praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} and {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|WD=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|WD=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}} ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|lt=Sanuki Shiroyama Castle|1=Kiyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{ill|lt=hammered stone tools|Q1142470|WD=Q1142470|lt=stone tools|1=Lithic reduction}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for rain here. * {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=King Kamikushi Tomb|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} ** Location: {{ill|lt=Takamatsu City|Q200707|WD=Q200707|1=Takamatsu}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Prince Kamikushi|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{ill|lt=circular tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} ({{ill|lt=Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750|WD=Q7456750|1=Seto-Ōhashi Line}}) {{ill|lt=Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413|WD=Q5115413|1=Sanuki-Fuchū Station}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} - {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|WD=Q349287|lt=Retired Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|1=Kiyama Castle}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nakatado District|Q1206155|WD=Q1206155|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kotohira Town|Q1271483|WD=Q1271483|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}Shimokushiri) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}. The enshrined deity is also Kushikushio no Mikoto. === External link === {{Commonscat|Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiyama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の神社]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakaide]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakaide, Kagawa]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Sanuki Province]] [[Category:香川県の旧県社]] [[Category:讃岐氏|社きやましんしや さかいてし]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

{{ill|Mount Kiyama|lt=Mount Kiyama|ceb|Ki Yama|WD=Q31699082|1=Mount Kiyama}} == image of entrance (P9721) == * Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide)}} [[JA:城山神社 (坂出市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11426120}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shiroyama Shrine |Image = [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), haiden.jpg|280px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|17|38.50|N|133|54|40.70|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shiroyama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Kamikushi Oji|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}8 years |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Kasugazo|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = {{ill|lt=October 8th|Q2924|WD=Q2924|1=October 8}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Kagawa|label=Shiroyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Kiyama-jinja (Sakaide), torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shiroyama Shrine'' (Kiyama Shrine) is located in Fuchu Town, {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are enshrined. ; Main enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} :: In some literature, he is also written as ``Kamikushi no Miko.'' He was the {{ill|lt=prince|prince|WD=prince|ltq=Q12886381}} of the 12th {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} and the ancestor of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}}. ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} :* {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenminakanushikami|1=UNKNOWN}} === History === [[File:Myojin-bara Ruins in Kiyama.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Myojinbara Ruins}}]] === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, the enshrined deity {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} was appointed {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} for his work in exterminating evil fish in the southern seas. He later built a mansion in Shiroyama and lived there, but died at the age of 120 in {{ill|lt=Emperor Chuai|Q179971|WD=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}. Later, a shrine was built to enshrine the divine spirit, and this is the beginning of our company. ``Sanuki Kokukansha Kosho'' (from the Shiroyama Shrine section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture''). Shrine history book. .Regarding {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}" also says, "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kamikushio no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (same as Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto) In the Nihon Shoki, in the 4th year of Emperor Keiko's reign, it is written, ``Next wife Isokawahime, Prince Ikami Kushi, Prince Inase Irihiko.Her older brother Prince Kamikushi, also the founder of the creation of Sanuki Province.'' . In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Sendai Kojihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}" and "{{ill|lt=Kokuzohonki|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}," "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Suri Hore no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, the grandson of Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto III, was the son of Sanuki Kunizo in the {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} period. Kokuzōhonki ({{ill|lt=Sendai Kūjihonki|Q2716751|WD=Q2716751|1=Kujiki}}, Vol. 10) Sanuki Kunizojo ``{{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Karushima Toyomincho|1=Emperor Ōjin}} reign.Emperor Keiko, Kamikushio III, Grandson, Noriyoshi no Mikoto, and Kunizo.'' , {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kunizo|Q21653200|WD=Q21653200|1=sanukinokuninomiyatsuko}} are the same in that they are the ancestors of the clan. Also, according to the shrine's legend, it was originally enshrined at the Myojinbara Ruins ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City)#Related Sites|Q11426120|WD=Q11426120|lt=described later|1=Kiyama Shrine}}) which remains on one peak of {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}}. Since there are still several megaliths in Myojinhara, it is assumed that rituals have been held there since ancient times. . === Outline === In {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|WD=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, "{{ill|lt=Japan Three Dynasties Annals|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}" {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} 1st Year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}) Article (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In the 7th year ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}) article, there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} to "Shiroyama Kami". {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shinmeicho|Q11064932|Shinmeicho}}, {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ano District|Q11658001|WD=Q11658001|1=Aya district}} "Shiroyama Shrine" It is listed as ``Meishin Taisha'' and is listed as {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}. In addition to our company, there are only three {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Sanuki Country|Q929682|WD=Q929682|1=Sanuki Province}}: {{ill|lt=Tamura Shrine|Q11576976|WD=Q11576976|1=Tamura Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Awai Shrine|Q11604560|WD=Q11604560|1=Awai Shrine}}. The reason why it was revered in this way seems to have been related to the fact that {{ill|lt=Sanuki Kokubunji|Q1101226|WD=Q1101226|lt=Sanuki Kokufu|1=Sanuki Kokubun-ji}} was located at the foot of Mt. Shiroyama , and there is also a theory that it served as the protector of the prefecture and the provincial governor .According to "Sugaya Bunso", in {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}}4 ({{ill|lt=888|Q29956|WD=Q29956|1=AD 888}}), {{ill|lt=Sugawara Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} of {{ill|lt=Sanuki Province|Q929682|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} built a castle. Praying for rain at the Yama Shrine "{{ill|lt=Sugaya Bunso|Q109359816|WD=Q109359816|1=Kanke Bunsō}}" Shiroyama Shinbun Festival held on May 6, 1947 ([http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/550401.html Shiroyama Shrine] (see Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection). . It is said that the prayer was successful and brought about a bountiful harvest. The state of the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}} is unknown, but it appears to have gradually declined, and then in the first year of {{ill|lt=Sadaharu|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} ({{jalink|13 It was destroyed by fire during the battle between {{ill|lt=Yoriyuki Hosokawa|Q1139739|WD=Q1139739|1=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|lt=Kiyoshi Hosokawa|Q11605934|WD=Q11605934|lt=Kiyoshi|1=Hosokawa Kiyouji}} in 1962|Q6166}}.. Therefore, only the divine object was moved to the land of Inna ({{Coord|34|17|43.51|N|133|55|03.35|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Inna (temporary transfer location)}}), a small shrine was built, and it was later moved to the current location . After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was ranked as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}} in {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} November 7th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from upper 6th rank to lower rank of junior 5th rank (``{{ill|lt=Jihon Sandai Jiroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' *October 9, 7th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865|Q30350|WD=Q30350|1=865}}), the first rank of Jugoi (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') - Written as ``Shiroyama no Kami.'' === Precincts === File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Shrine.JPG|Shrine File: Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) front of the worship hall.JPG|worship hall == Setsusha == '''Precincts end shrine''' [[File:Shiroyama Shrine (Sakaide City) Precincts Amake Tenmangu.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Amake Tenmangu}}]] * Amaketenmangu Shrine *: The enshrined deity is {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. {{ill|lt=Ninwa|Q1346317|WD=Q1346317|1=Ninna}} It began with Michizane's {{ill|lt=rain praying|Q477738|WD=Q477738|1=Rainmaking (ritual)}}, which was worshiped in gratitude. * Four temples '''Outside shrine''' * Fukumiya Shrine * Goryo Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Susanoosha * Ichikishimasha * Sunano Shrine === Related locations === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama (Kagawa Prefecture)|UNKNOWN|lt=Shiroyama}} (Kiyama) *: A mountain with an altitude of 462m located between {{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}} and {{ill|lt=Marugame City|Q460835|WD=Q460835|1=Marugame, Kagawa}}, rising behind Shiroyama Shrine. It is said that {{ill|lt=ancient mountain castle|Q6431764|WD=Q6431764|1=ancient mountain fortress in Japan}} ({{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|lt=Sanuki Shiroyama Castle|1=Kiyama Castle}}) was built on top of the mountain, and the mountain name ``Shiroyama'' is derived from this. The remains of castles can be seen throughout the area, and are designated as national historic sites. . In addition, {{ill|lt=hammered stone tools|Q1142470|WD=Q1142470|lt=stone tools|1=Lithic reduction}} were also excavated near the summit of the mountain. It is known that people have lived there since ancient times. It was also used as a place for rituals, as shown in the Myojingahara ruins below. * Myojinbaru Ruins ** Location: Southeast peak of Shiroyama ({{Coord|34|17|33.65|N|133|53|57.83|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Myojinbaru ruins (former enshrinement site)}})*: It is said to be the former enshrinement site of Shiroyama Shrine. Because it is dotted with huge stones, it is thought to have been a sacred place since ancient times as a place where gods descended, and it is speculated that rituals have been held here since ancient times. It is also said that {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} prayed for rain here. * {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi#Tomb|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=King Kamikushi Tomb|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}} ** Location: {{ill|lt=Takamatsu City|Q200707|WD=Q200707|1=Takamatsu}}Mure Town, Mure ({{Coord|34|20|35.28|N|134|07|17.22|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Kamikushio Official Tomb}}) *: {{ill|lt=Prince Kamikushi|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Prince Kamikushi|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}, which is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency. * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} ** Location: Fuchu-cho, Sakaide City ({{Coord|34|17|35.80|N|133|54|59.45|E|region:JP-37_type:landmark|name=Tsumioka Shrine (Kamikushio Tomb?)}}) *: According to the Shiroyama Shrine biography, the site of Tsuzumioka Shrine is {{ill|lt=keyhole-shaped tumulus|Q11268718|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, and is also said to be the tomb of {{ill|lt=Kamikushi no Mikoto|Q11590432|WD=Q11590432|lt=Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto|1=Kamukushi no Mikoto}}In the Tsuzumioka section of ``Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture'', it is written as {{ill|lt=circular tumulus|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} (the relationship with Kamikushibetsu no Mikoto is not pointed out). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|Q161454|WD=Q161454|1=Kagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakaide City|Q855059|WD=Q855059|1=Sakaide, Kagawa}}4760 Honmura, Fuchu-cho '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=Shikoku Railway|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}} (JR Shikoku) {{ill|lt=Yosan Line|Q575016|WD=Q575016|1=Yosan Line}} ({{ill|lt=Seto Ohashi Line|Q7456750|WD=Q7456750|1=Seto-Ōhashi Line}}) {{ill|lt=Sanuki Fuchu Station|Q5115413|WD=Q5115413|1=Sanuki-Fuchū Station}} (about 20 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * Sanuki Kokufu Ruins * {{ill|lt=Tsumioka Shrine|Q11678981|WD=Q11678981|1=Tsuzumigaoka Shrine}} - {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|WD=Q349287|lt=Retired Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}'s place of worship. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book * "Japanese Historical Place Name System: Place Names of Kagawa Prefecture" ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}) Sakaide City Shiroyama Section/Shiroyama Shrine Section * Edited by Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, "Kagawa Prefecture Shrine Journal" Volume 2, Kagawa Prefectural Shinto Priests Association, 1932. National Diet Library Digital Collection https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1227849 (Reference 2025-06-30) === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Shiroyama Castle (Sanuki Province)|Q46999719|WD=Q46999719|1=Kiyama Castle}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushinashi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nakatado District|Q1206155|WD=Q1206155|1=Nakatado District, Kagawa}}{{ill|lt=Kotohira Town|Q1271483|WD=Q1271483|1=Kotohira, Kagawa}}Shimokushiri) - {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}.
